title: GRAPH_MIDDLEWARE_CHECKPOINTER_INTERRUPT_DIAGNOSTIC

pressure_assessment.reason: Read-only diagnostic scan only — no code execution, no API calls, no tools, no side effects

lane_0_pressure_assessment.r139_outcome: Outcome B2 — still interrupted (WITH model call)
lane_0_pressure_assessment.rationale: R139 confirmed interrupt fires AFTER model response; model was called; next diagnostic must trace exact causal chain

lane_1_r139_outcome_deconstruction.key_observation: Model response was published to stream bridge BEFORE the interrupt. abort_event.is_set() returned False during model response publish, but True at final status check.
lane_1_r139_outcome_deconstruction.implication: abort_event was set AFTER the model response was published and BEFORE the worker loop exited normally.

lane_2_worker_astream_loop_analysis.loop_structure: Single-mode astream loop (worker.py:233-241): async for chunk in agent.astream(...): if record.abort_event.is_set(): break; sse_event = _lg_mode_to_sse_event(single_mode); await bridge.publish(run_id, sse_event, serialize(chunk, mode=single_mode))

publish_order_analysis.event: model response published to stream bridge
publish_order_analysis.abort_event_state: False (otherwise loop would break before publish)

publish_order_analysis.event: loop continues or exits normally (no more chunks)

lane_2_worker_astream_loop_analysis.critical_finding: The abort_event was set AFTER model response published (step 1) and BEFORE final status check (step 3). The only way abort_event is set is via: (a) manager.cancel(), (b) create_or_reject interrupt/rollback cleanup, or (c) something external.

two_possible_timeline_reconstructions.scenario: Scenario A: abort_event set DURING model API call
two_possible_timeline_reconstructions.timeline: worker:running → model API starts → model API completes (response published) → abort_event set → loop exits normally → interrupted status
two_possible_timeline_reconstructions.abortion_time: after model response, before loop completion
two_possible_timeline_reconstructions.consistent_with_evidence: model_response_detected=True (abort_event False during publish), final_status=interrupted (abort_event True at final check)

two_possible_timeline_reconstructions.scenario: Scenario B: abort_event set by external cancellation during astream
two_possible_timeline_reconstructions.timeline: worker:running → astream starts → model response published → external task.cancel() called → abort_event.set() → loop breaks or exits → interrupted status
two_possible_timeline_reconstructions.abortion_time: after model response, during continued astream processing
two_possible_timeline_reconstructions.consistent_with_evidence: CancelledError caught by worker.py:283, model_response_detected=True, final_status=interrupted

cancel_method_analysis.r139_relevance: NOT called in R139 — stream endpoint never invoked, sse_consumer never runs

create_or_reject_interrupt_analysis.condition: multitask_strategy in ('interrupt', 'rollback') AND inflight runs exist
create_or_reject_interrupt_analysis.r139_relevance: NOT triggered in R139 — multitask_strategy='reject' (default) and no inflight runs exist for R139's thread

lane_3_manager_cancel_and_create_or_reje.conclusion: Neither cancel() nor create_or_reject interrupt path is in R139's call path. Yet task.cancel() was definitely called (CancelledError caught). Something else must be calling task.cancel().

endpoint_inaccessibility.sse_consumer_function: services.py:296 — async generator, finally block: if on_disconnect == cancel and status in (pending, running): await run_mgr.cancel(record.run_id)
endpoint_inaccessibility.conclusion: sse_consumer's cancel path is IMPOSSIBLE to reach in R139. on_disconnect cancel requires (a) stream endpoint called, (b) SSE client disconnected, (c) on_disconnect == cancel. None of these apply to R139.

on_disconnect_field_analysis.r139_payload: interrupt_before=[] interrupt_after=[] — on_disconnect uses default 'cancel' but stream endpoint never called
on_disconnect_field_analysis.relevance: No impact — stream endpoint not called

worker.py:262_logic.key_insight: This check is at line 262, OUTSIDE the astream loop (post-loop). It fires AFTER the loop exhausts all chunks OR breaks due to abort_event being set.
worker.py:262_logic.r139_model_response_published: stream_event_count=2 confirms at least one chunk published after model response
worker.py:262_logic.loop_exit_behavior: Loop exits either (a) naturally when graph finishes, or (b) via break when abort_event.is_set() during iteration
worker.py:262_logic.evidence: model_response_detected=True → abort_event.is_set() returned False at least once during model response publish

worker.py:283_cancelled_error_handler.action_path: action == 'rollback' → error status; else → interrupted status + logger.info('Run %s was cancelled', run_id)
worker.py:283_cancelled_error_handler.r139_log_evidence: worker.py:301 log line 'was cancelled' confirms CancelledError WAS caught — task.cancel() was called
worker.py:283_cancelled_error_handler.task_cancel_source: task.cancel() only called at manager.py:162 (cancel method) and manager.py:206 (create_or_reject interrupt/rollback cleanup)

lane_5_worker_astream_completion_logic.critical_timeline_gap: task.cancel() was called in R139 but neither manager.cancel() nor create_or_reject interrupt/rollback is in R139's call path. Something is calling task.cancel() that is NOT in the standard manager cancel path.

lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.hypothesis: Cause A — TestClient StreamingResponse / response handling triggering background task cancellation
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.testclient_response_lifecycle: POST /runs endpoint (create_run) returns RunResponse immediately WITHOUT streaming. The response is a plain JSON HTTP response (RunResponse model), not a StreamingResponse. Therefore, the response itself does NOT trigger any stream cleanup.
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.fastapi_testclient_under_app_lifespan: Each TestClient(app) context starts a fresh lifespan. Background tasks created via asyncio.create_task() during request handling outlive the request/response cycle if not awaited within the request handler.
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.services.py:272_task_creation: task = asyncio.create_task(run_agent(...)); record.task = task. HTTP response returns immediately after create_run() returns.
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.response_type_for_create_run: create_run() returns RunResponse (JSON), not StreamingResponse. No SSE stream involved.
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.key_finding: POST /runs (create_run) returns a regular JSON response (RunResponse), NOT a StreamingResponse. The StreamingResponse is only used by POST /runs/stream endpoint. Therefore, there is NO stream cleanup that could trigger sse_consumer's cancel path.
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.alternative_testclient_hypothesis: TestClient may cancel background tasks when the TestClient context exits (at the end of the 'with' block). This would happen AFTER the HTTP response is returned and the test polls for status. If the TestClient's event loop is torn down while run_agent is still running, task.cancel() would be called, triggering CancelledError in worker.
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.test_hypothesis_mechanism: with TestClient(app) as client: client.post('/runs', json=payload) → returns immediately with run_id; test then polls status; then 'with' block exits → TestClient tears down lifespan/event loop → background run_agent task cancelled → CancelledError caught → status=interrupted
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.r139_polling_timing: poll_elapsed_seconds=3. The test polled until completion. If TestClient context exited during or shortly after polling, the background task could be cancelled.
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.evidence_for: Explains why CancelledError was caught despite no explicit cancel() call
lane_6_testclient_background_task_event_.evidence_against: Would apply to ALL tests using TestClient background tasks, not just R139

lane_7_streambridge_drain_and_stream_cle.hypothesis: Cause B — StreamBridge stream object lifecycle and _run_complete() signal
lane_7_streambridge_drain_and_stream_cle.not_investigated: Full StreamBridge cleanup path (was cut off from analysis due to context limits — pending LANE 7-8 completion)
lane_7_streambridge_drain_and_stream_cle.key_question: Does MemoryStreamBridge have any cleanup that could trigger abort_event or task.cancel()?

lane_8_checkpointer_interrupt_state_hypo.hypothesis: Cause D — LangGraph checkpointer having interrupt state from prior execution
lane_8_checkpointer_interrupt_state_hypo.not_investigated: Full checkpointer interrupt analysis — pending LANE 7-8 completion
lane_8_checkpointer_interrupt_state_hypo.key_question: Could an old checkpoint with interrupt_before_nodes set cause a new run to interrupt?

lane_9_diagnostic_conclusion.most_likely_cause: Cause A — TestClient background task cancellation via event loop lifecycle
evidence: task.cancel() was called (CancelledError caught in worker.py:301)
Neither manager.cancel() nor create_or_reject interrupt path in R139 call path
POST /runs returns plain JSON (RunResponse), not StreamingResponse — no SSE stream cleanup
TestClient lifecycle teardown could cancel background tasks when context exits
3-second poll_elapsed is consistent with TestClient context exiting during polling
lane_9_diagnostic_conclusion.alternative_cause: Cause C — StreamingResponse returned from POST /runs with no actual SSE stream, but some cleanup path triggered
lane_9_diagnostic_conclusion.alternative_evidence: create_run() returns RunResponse, not StreamingResponse — POST /runs does not involve SSE stream handling
lane_9_diagnostic_conclusion.cause_b_evidence: stream_event_count=2 (model response published), abort_event.is_set() returned False during model response, then True at final check — suggests abort_event.set() called BETWEEN model response publish and worker loop completion
lane_9_diagnostic_conclusion.cause_d_evidence: R139 used new run_id and new thread_id — checkpointer interrupt state from prior execution unlikely
lane_9_diagnostic_conclusion.primary_recommendation: R139C must test: disable asyncio.create_task — await run_agent directly instead — to see if interrupt still fires. If interrupt disappears when run_agent is awaited inline (not background), Cause A (TestClient lifecycle) is confirmed.

lane_10_r140_plan_generation.title: INTERRUPT_CAUSAL_CHAIN_TEST
lane_10_r140_plan_generation.objective: Test the causal chain between TestClient background task lifecycle and the interrupt. Specifically: await run_agent inline (not as background task) and see if interrupt still occurs.
lane_10_r140_plan_generation.approach: Modify POST /runs to await run_agent inline instead of asyncio.create_task — eliminates background task that could be cancelled by TestClient lifecycle
lane_10_r140_plan_generation.expected_outcome: If interrupt still fires with inline await → interrupt source is in graph/middleware/checkpointer, NOT TestClient lifecycle. If interrupt disappears with inline await → Cause A (TestClient background task cancellation) confirmed.

existing_unknowns_from_r138_r139.description: interrupt_before=[] and interrupt_after=[] passed — interrupt PERSISTS
existing_unknowns_from_r138_r139.updated: CONFIRMED: interrupt persists with interrupt_before=[]; now confirmed interrupt fires AFTER model response
narrowed to: (A) TestClient lifecycle cancellation, (B) StreamBridge cleanup, (C) Unknown middleware, (D) Checkpointer interrupt

new_unknowns.description: task.cancel() was called in R139 (CancelledError caught) but neither manager.cancel() nor create_or_reject interrupt path is in R139 call path
create_run() returns RunResponse (JSON), not StreamingResponse — no SSE stream cleanup should be involved, yet interrupt fires

critical_finding: Model response was published (103 chars, 2 events, stream_event_count=2) before the interrupt. abort_event.is_set() returned False during model response publish, then True at final status check. task.cancel() was definitely called (CancelledError caught at worker.py:301). Neither manager.cancel() nor create_or_reject interrupt/rollback is in R139's call path. POST /runs returns plain JSON (RunResponse), not StreamingResponse. Most likely cause: TestClient background task cancellation during event loop lifecycle teardown (Cause A), OR an unknown cleanup path in the run_agent completion path (Cause C).
